senses.definition: a young individual with little or no experience

examples.x: Michael had been a spring chicken in the realm of coding until he attended coding boot camp.
Olivia is exploring different musical instruments; she's a spring chicken in the world of music.

senses.definition: a young energetic person that brings new ideas, enthusiasm, and vitality to a group, organization, or activity

examples.x: Olivia's enthusiasm and passion for environmental causes make her the young blood that inspires others to take action.
The company hired some young blood to revitalize its marketing strategies and bring a modern perspective to the campaigns.
The aging nonprofit organization welcomed young blood on its board, hoping for innovative ideas to adapt to changing societal needs.
The sports team introduced young blood to its roster, injecting new energy and enthusiasm into the game.
The tech startup thrived on the creativity of its young blood, constantly pushing boundaries and staying ahead in the industry.

senses.definition: used when someone has learned or experienced something from a very young age

examples.x: Charlotte excels in playing the piano because she learned the instrument at her mother's knee.
While growing up, he had been exposed to various musical genres at his mother's knee, developing a diverse appreciation for music.

senses.definition: (of a person) very young or very small

examples.x: When I first met him, he was knee-high to a grasshopper, and now he's a towering basketball player.
I've known her since she was knee-high to a grasshopper; it's incredible to see how she's grown.
Grandpa used to tell stories about when he was knee-high to a grasshopper, walking to school in the snow.
The antique chair has been in the family since I was knee-high to a grasshopper.
Back when we were knee-high to a grasshopper, we used to play in the fields behind our house for hours.

senses.definition: the time in a person's life marked by youthfulness, inexperience, and being carefree

examples.x: During his college years, Mark enjoyed the freedom and carefree nature of the salad days, spending long nights studying, socializing with friends, and exploring new experiences.
Lisa is currently in her salad days, enjoying her carefree college life and exploring new adventures with her friends.
The group of teenagers is savoring their salad days, making memories and embracing the joy of youth before responsibilities take over.

word_list.pronunciation: ɑːnðə ˈʌðɚ ɹˈɔŋ sˈaɪd ʌv nˈʌm

senses.definition: used to refer to people who look older than the age they are at
an advanced stage of life characterized by a long lifespan and great wisdom

examples.x: At a ripe old age, Mary remains active and engaged in various hobbies and interests, embracing life to the fullest.
Sarah's grandparents have reached a ripe old age and continue to enjoy each other's company after decades of marriage.

senses.definition: used to describe someone or something that is old and has existed for a long time

examples.x: Grandma's rocking chair is old as the hills, passed down through generations, its sturdy frame and well-worn seat holding cherished memories.
Martha, who is as old as the hills, shares her stories of the past with great nostalgia and wisdom.
The ancient oak tree stands tall and strong, a testament to its age as old as the hills.

senses.definition: describing an individual who has lived for a very long time and is not able to do certain activities due to old age

examples.x: The car is getting a bit long in the tooth, but it still runs well.
Although he's long in the tooth, his experience makes him invaluable to the team.
That software is long in the tooth and needs an update to stay competitive.
She may be long in the tooth, but her wisdom and insights are unmatched.
The house is long in the tooth, but it has a lot of character and charm.

senses.definition: used to describe an old person who is still very active and healthy

examples.x: Despite his age, Grandpa Joe is hale and hearty, waking up early every morning for his daily jog and enjoying an active lifestyle.
The team's star player returned to the field, hale and hearty, after recovering from an injury, scoring a winning goal in the championship match.

senses.definition: a senior citizen that spends a lot of time using the Internet

examples.x: Mark, a retired gentleman, has embraced technology and become a silver surfer, exploring the internet, connecting with friends on social media, and discovering new hobbies online.
The retirement community organized a workshop to teach seniors how to become silver surfers, empowering them to navigate the internet, shop online, and access information at their fingertips.

word_list.pronunciation: ˈoʊld ɪnˈʌf təbi wˈʌnz mˈʌðɚ fˈɑːðɚ

senses.definition: used to refer to a significant age difference between two people, especially those who are romantically involved with one another

examples.x: As Jessica watched her son's new girlfriend interact with their family, she couldn't help but feel a bit uncomfortable, considering that the girl appeared to be old enough to be his mother.
Sarah's new partner was much older than her, appearing to be old enough to be her father, which led to some questioning looks and conversations among their friends.

senses.definition: beyond the peak of one's abilities or career, often implying a decline in performance

examples.x: He joked that he was over the hill when he turned fifty.
Some believe they are over the hill at forty, but age is just a number.
She felt over the hill when she couldn't keep up with her younger colleagues.
They teased him about being over the hill after he struggled during the hike.
Despite feeling over the hill, she completed the marathon with determination.
